# Frequently Asked Questions — Local AI Assistant

## General Questions

### What is Local AI Assistant?
Local AI Assistant is a desktop application that indexes your business documents (policies, procedures, pricing, templates, and FAQs) and lets you search them using AI-powered semantic search. All processing happens locally on your machine — no data is sent to the cloud.

### Is my data private and secure?
Yes. All document processing, text extraction, and embedding generation happen entirely on your local device. We do not store, transmit, or access your documents on any external server. Your data stays on your machine.

### What file types are supported?
We currently support:
- `.txt` — plain text files
- `.pdf` — PDF documents (text-based)
- `.docx` — Microsoft Word documents

### What embedding models are available?
The default is `nomic-embed-text`, which is free and runs locally. You can configure other embedding-capable models in the settings. The model must be available in LM Studio or Ollama.

## Technical Questions

### What are the system requirements?
- **Operating System**: Windows 10/11, macOS 12+, or Linux
- **RAM**: Minimum 8 GB (16 GB recommended)
- **Storage**: 2 GB free for the application + model storage
- **Internet**: Only needed for initial setup and model downloads
- **GPU**: Optional — models can run on CPU

### How does the search work?
1. Your documents are split into ~500-token chunks
2. Each chunk is converted to a vector embedding by a local AI model
3. When you search, your query is also converted to an embedding
4. The system calculates cosine similarity between your query and all chunks
5. The most similar chunks are returned as results

### How often should I re-index my documents?
Run the indexer whenever you add, remove, or modify documents. The indexer uses file hashing to skip unchanged files, so re-indexing is fast even with large document sets.

documents. The indexer uses file hashing to skip unchanged files, so re-indexing is fast even with large document sets.

### Can I use this without LM Studio?
You can use keyword-only search without any local server. However, for semantic (AI-powered) search, you need LM Studio running with an embedding model loaded.

## Support Questions

### How do I get help?
- Email: support@localaiassistant.com
- In-app: Help → Contact Support
- Documentation: docs.localaiassistant.com

### How do I uninstall?
Simply delete the application folder. All your indexed data is stored in `embeddings.json` inside the application directory. You can keep this file to preserve your index if you reinstall.

# Employee Code of Conduct

Last Updated: February 1, 2025

## 1. Purpose

This Code of Conduct establishes the standards of behavior expected of all employees, contractors, and representatives of Local AI Assistant. It reflects our core values and commitment to integrity, respect, and accountability.

## 2. Core Values

### Integrity
We conduct business with the highest ethical standards. Honesty and transparency are expected in all interactions.

### Respect
We treat everyone with dignity and respect, regardless of position, background, or identity.

### Accountability
We take responsibility for our actions and decisions. We learn from mistakes and strive to improve.

### Innovation
We embrace creative thinking and continuous improvement in everything we do.

## 3. Workplace Standards

### Professional Behavior
- Maintain professionalism in all communications and interactions
- Resolve conflicts constructively and through appropriate channels
- Avoid harassment, discrimination, or intimidation of any kind
- Dress appropriately for the work environment

### Confidentiality
- Protect sensitive company and client information
- Do not share internal documents without authorization
- Follow data classification guidelines when handling information
- Report any suspected data breaches immediately

### Conflicts of Interest
- Disclose any personal relationships that may create conflicts
- Avoid outside employment that competes with the company
- Decline gifts or favors that could influence business decisions
- Report potential conflicts to your manager or HR

## 4. Compliance

All employees must comply with applicable laws and regulations, including:

- Data protection and privacy laws
- Labor and employment regulations
- Industry-specific compliance requirements
- Company policies and procedures

## 5. Reporting Violations

If you observe or experience a violation of this code:

licies and procedures

## 5. Reporting Violations

If you observe or experience a violation of this code:

1. Report to your direct manager
2. Contact HR at hr@localaiassistant.com
3. Use the anonymous reporting hotline if preferred
4. No retaliation will occur for good-faith reports

## 6. Disciplinary Actions

Violations may result in:

# Privacy Policy

Last Updated: January 15, 2025

## 1. Introduction

Welcome to Local AI Assistant. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you use our services.

## 2. Information We Collect

We collect the following types of information:

- **Personal Information**: Name, email address, and phone number when you create an account.
- **Usage Data**: Information about how you interact with our application, including search queries and document uploads.
- **Device Information**: IP address, browser type, operating system, and device identifiers.
- **Document Content**: Any documents you upload for indexing and processing. We process this content locally on your machine.

## 3. How We Use Your Information

Your information is used to:

- Provide, maintain, and improve our services
- Process and index your documents locally
- Respond to your support requests
- Send you service-related notifications
- Ensure the security and integrity of our platform

## 4. Data Storage and Security

All your data is stored locally on your device. We do not send your documents or personal information to any external servers. The local AI models run entirely on your hardware.

We implement the following security measures:

- Local-only data storage
- Encrypted file storage where supported
- Regular security updates
- No third-party data sharing

## 5. Your Rights

You have the right to:

- Access your personal data at any time
- Request correction of inaccurate data
- Request deletion of your data
- Export your data in a portable format
- Withdraw consent at any time

## 6. Contact

# Service Pricing — Local AI Assistant

Effective: April 1, 2025

## Individual Plan

**$19/month** or **$190/year** (save 17%)

- Single user license
- Up to 500 documents indexed
- Local embedding model (nomic-embed-text)
- Basic search and retrieval
- Email support

## Team Plan

**$79/month** or **$790/year** (save 17%)

- Up to 10 users
- Up to 5,000 documents indexed
- Choice of embedding models
- Semantic and keyword search
- Priority email support
- Admin dashboard

## Enterprise Plan

**Custom Pricing**

- Unlimited users
- Unlimited documents
- Custom embedding models
- API access
- Dedicated support engineer
- SLA guarantees
- On-premise deployment option
- Custom integrations

## Add-ons

- **Additional Storage**: $10/month per 1,000 documents
- **Premium Models**: $15/month for advanced embedding models
- **API Access**: $25/month for programmatic search
- **Custom Training**: Starting at $500 one-time fee

## Payment Terms

- All plans billed monthly or annually
- 30-day money-back guarantee
- Annual plans billed upfront
- Volume discounts available for 50+ users

## Refund Policy

# IT Incident Response Procedure

Version: 2.1 | Effective: January 1, 2025

## 1. Purpose

This procedure defines how Local AI Assistant responds to IT incidents, including security breaches, system outages, and data loss events.

## 2. Incident Severity Levels

### Severity 1 — Critical
- Complete system outage affecting all users
- Active security breach with data exfiltration confirmed
- Financial or legal impact exceeding $100,000
- **Response Time**: Immediate (within 15 minutes)

### Severity 2 — High
- Partial system outage affecting critical services
- Suspected security incident requiring investigation
- Customer data at risk but no confirmed breach
- **Response Time**: Within 1 hour

### Severity 3 — Medium
- Non-critical system degradation
- Isolated security events with limited impact
- Internal tool outages affecting a single department
- **Response Time**: Within 4 hours

### Severity 4 — Low
- Minor bugs or configuration issues
- Single-user access problems
- Non-urgent security advisories
- **Response Time**: Within 24 hours

## 3. Incident Response Steps

### Step 1: Detection and Reporting
- Any employee can report an incident via:
  - Email: incidents@localaiassistant.com
  - Slack: #incident-response channel
  - Phone: IT emergency line ext. 9111

### Step 2: Triage and Classification
- Security team assesses severity level
- Assigns incident commander
- Determines initial containment strategy

### Step 3: Containment
- Isolate affected systems from the network
- Preserve evidence and logs
- Communicate status to stakeholders
- Begin root cause analysis

### Step 4: Eradication
- Remove threat or fix vulnerability
- Patch affected systems
- Verify no remaining threat actors or backdoors

### Step 5: Recovery
- Restore systems from clean backups
- Verify data integrity
- Gradually return services to production
- Monitor for recurrence

rify data integrity
- Gradually return services to production
- Monitor for recurrence

### Step 6: Post-Incident Review
- Conduct blameless post-mortem within 5 business days
- Document timeline, root cause, and lessons learned
- Create action items with owners and deadlines
- Update procedures and defenses as needed

## 4. Communication Protocol

### Internal Communication
- Status updates every 30 minutes for S1 incidents
- Status updates every 2 hours for S2 incidents
- Daily updates for S3 incidents
- Weekly updates for S4 incidents

### External Communication
- Customer notifications handled by Customer Success
- Legal team consulted for any data breach
- PR team engaged for public-facing incidents
- No external communication without executive approval

## 5. Escalation Path

1. On-call engineer (first responder)
2. Senior security engineer
3. IT Director
4. CTO / VP of Engineering
5. CEO (for S1 incidents with business impact)

## 6. Recovery Time Objectives

# Employee Onboarding Procedure

Version: 3.2 | Effective: March 1, 2025

## Overview

This procedure outlines the steps for onboarding new employees at Local AI Assistant. The process typically spans the first two weeks of employment.

## Pre-Arrival (3 Days Before Start Date)

1. **IT Setup**
   - Create email account and Slack workspace invitation
   - Provision laptop and necessary software licenses
   - Set up access to internal systems (GitHub, Jira, Confluence)
   - Prepare security badges and building access

2. **HR Preparation**
   - Complete employment paperwork (W-4, I-9, direct deposit)
   - Enroll in benefits packages
   - Schedule orientation session
   - Assign an onboarding buddy

## Day 1 — Welcome Day

### Morning (9:00 AM - 12:00 PM)
- Welcome meeting with HR and team lead
- Office tour and introductions to key team members
- IT setup assistance and equipment handover
- Review of company handbook and policies

### Afternoon (1:00 PM - 5:00 PM)
- Team lunch with your department
- Introduction to core tools and systems
- Review of role expectations and key performance indicators
- Set up 30-60-90 day goals with your manager

## Week 1 — Immersion

### Daily Activities
- Complete mandatory compliance training modules
- Shadow team members on key workflows
- Attend team standups and sprint planning sessions
- Read key project documentation and architecture guides

### Day 3 Check-in
- One-on-one with your manager to address questions
- Review initial goals and adjust as needed
- Ensure all access and tools are working properly

### Day 5 Check-in
- Feedback session with your onboarding buddy
- Review first week experience
- Identify any blockers or additional support needed

## Week 2 — Active Contribution

### Activities
- Begin contributing to actual projects with guidance
- Attend cross-functional meetings relevant to your role
- Complete any remaining training modules
- Participate in your first sprint planning

rojects with guidance
- Attend cross-functional meetings relevant to your role
- Complete any remaining training modules
- Participate in your first sprint planning

### Day 10 Check-in
- Formal check-in with HR and manager
- Review progress against 30-60-90 day goals
- Provide onboarding experience feedback
- Confirm benefits enrollment and any remaining paperwork

## 30-60-90 Day Plan

### 30 Days — Learning
- Understand team processes and tools
- Complete all mandatory training
- Build relationships across the team
- Ask questions and seek feedback

### 60 Days — Contributing
- Take ownership of small projects or tasks
- Begin making independent decisions within scope
- Identify areas for professional development
- Provide constructive feedback to the team

### 90 Days — Independent
- Fully integrated into team workflows
- Operating independently in core responsibilities
- Identified and begun addressing development goals
- Ready for formal performance review

## Support Resources

# Project Kickoff Checklist

**Project Name:** _______________
**Project Manager:** _______________
**Kickoff Date:** _______________

## Pre-Kickoff

- [ ] Project charter drafted and approved
- [ ] Stakeholder list finalized
- [ ] Core team members identified
- [ ] Kickoff meeting scheduled (60-90 min)
- [ ] Project space created (GitHub repo, wiki, etc.)
- [ ] Initial timeline and milestones defined
- [ ] Budget approved
- [ ] Risk assessment completed

## Kickoff Meeting Agenda

### Part 1: Context (15 min)
- Business problem and opportunity
- Project goals and success criteria
- Scope overview (what's in and out of scope)
- Key stakeholders and their roles

### Part 2: Team & Process (15 min)
- Team structure and roles
- Communication channels and cadence
- Tooling and workflows
- Decision-making process

### Part 3: Planning (20 min)
- Timeline and major milestones
- Resource allocation
- Dependencies and constraints
- Initial risk register

### Part 4: Q&A (10 min)
- Open discussion
- Clarification of expectations

### Part 5: Next Steps (5 min)
- Immediate action items
- Next meeting scheduled
- Contact for questions

## Post-Kickoff

- [ ] Meeting notes distributed within 24 hours
- [ ] All team members have tool access
- [ ] First sprint/work cycle begins
- [ ] Stakeholder update sent
- [ ] Project tracking initialized
- [ ] Risk register updated with new risks

# Meeting Template — Weekly Team Standup

**Date:** _______________
**Attendees:** _______________
**Facilitator:** _______________

## 1. Check-in (5 min)
How is everyone doing? Quick round-robin: one word or short phrase.

## 2. Review of Last Week (10 min)
- What did we commit to last week?
- What was completed?
- What is still in progress?
- Any blockers that need attention?

## 3. This Week's Priorities (15 min)
Top 3 priorities for the team this week:
1. _________________________
2. _________________________
3. _________________________

Individual updates:
- [Name]: _________________________
- [Name]: _________________________
- [Name]: _________________________

## 4. Blockers & Risks (5 min)
- Any blockers that need escalation?
- Any risks to upcoming deadlines?
- Resources or decisions needed?

## 5. Action Items (5 min)
| Action | Owner | Due Date |
|--------|-------|----------|
|        |       |          |
|        |       |          |
|        |       |          |

## 6. Wrap-up (5 min)
- Any announcements or important reminders?
- Next meeting date and time?
